seo.title: Building Function calling vs JSON mode vs constrained dec...
seo.description: A step-by-step implementation guide for Function calling vs JSON mode vs constrained decoding with Google Gemini and Cloudflare Workers AI.

content.intro: This guide provides a step-by-step implementation strategy for building reliable structured JSON output pipelines using AI models. Focuses on schema validation, provider compatibility, and envelope handling for production-grade systems.
prerequisites: Cloudflare Workers AI or Google Gemini API key
Basic understanding of JSON schema specifications

steps.title: Define strict Zod schemas with versioning
steps.explanation: Create schema definitions that balance validation strength with forward compatibility. Use Zod's refine method for custom validation rules. Version schemas to enable gradual migration.

steps.title: Configure model-specific JSON mode parameters
steps.explanation: Set model-specific parameters for structured output. Gemini requires 'response_schema' parameter, while Cloudflare Workers AI uses 'response_format' with 'type' set to 'json'.
pitfalls: Forgetting to stringify schema definitions for model ingestion

steps.title: Implement envelope response parsing
steps.explanation: Create middleware to handle models that wrap content in meta/seo/content structures. Extract the core content field before validation.
pitfalls: Assuming consistent response structures across models
Missing error handling for malformed envelope patterns

steps.title: Validate output against schema before use
steps.explanation: Perform strict validation on model output using Zod. Capture and log validation errors for debugging. Use zod's safeParse method for error tracking.

steps.title: Create schema compatibility layer for providers
steps.explanation: Build a schema transformation layer to normalize differences between model providers. Map provider-specific schema features to a common subset.

content.conclusion: By following these steps, developers can build robust structured output pipelines that handle validation, envelope patterns, and provider differences. Regularly update schema versions and monitor validation failures to maintain output reliability.
